title: Hanguana malayana
intro: Hanguana malayana, also known as the water chestnut vine, is a plant species native to Southeast Asia that belongs to the family Hanguanaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including treating fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Bioactive constituents of Hanguana malayana include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used to make herbal teas, tinctures, and capsules.
taxonomy: Hanguana malayana is a species of flowering plant in the family Hanguanaceae. It is native to Southeast Asia. The plant is known to be part of the Hanguana genus, which is a small family of flowering plants. Hanguana malayana is often placed in the order Commelinales, along with other families such as Commelinaceae and Philydraceae. The classification of Hanguana malayana is still subject to ongoing research and possible updates in the field of botany.

It has been traditionally used in some parts of the world to treat various ailments, including fever and skin conditions. preparations: Hanguana malayana is a plant that has been traditionally used in Southeast Asian herbal medicine for its medicinal properties.
